fields.label: Damage Type Exclusions

log.why: Feign Death grants resistance to all damage except Psychic, which needs a typed exception list instead of prose-only handling.

fields.comment: Lists damage types excluded from a broad defensive damage entry. Use this when canonical prose says a target resists or is immune to a large damage set with named exceptions, such as `all damage except Psychic`. It maps to `effects[].excludedDamageType[]`. Purpose: Damage Type Exclusions exists so exception-based resistance/immunity is not flattened into description prose or misrepresented as unconditional all-damage protection. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-type exception mechanic.

fields.label: Defense Damage Type Source

log.why: Absorb Elements lists eligible damage types, but the actual resistance is copied from the triggering damage event.

fields.comment: Explains how to interpret defensive `Damage Type` entries. Use `listed` when every listed type applies directly, `triggering_damage_type` when the active type is copied from the damage event that triggered the spell, and `chosen_damage_type` when a caster or player chooses one type from the listed options. It maps to `effects[].damageTypeSource`. Purpose: Defense Damage Type Source prevents dynamic resistance from being mistaken for simultaneous resistance to every eligible listed type. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no defensive damage-type mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.listed: Every listed Damage Type applies directly.
acceptedValueNotes.triggering_damage_type: The active type is the damage type from the event that triggered the spell.
acceptedValueNotes.chosen_damage_type: The active type is selected by the caster/player from the eligible list.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The spell has no defensive damage-type source mechanic.

fields.label: Damage Reduction Dice

log.why: Resistance reduces qualifying damage by 1d4, which is not the same mechanic as resistance or immunity.

fields.comment: Stores the rolled amount subtracted from qualifying damage. Use this for damage-reduction mechanics such as `reduce the total damage taken by 1d4`; do not use it for normal resistance or immunity. It maps to `effects[].damageReduction.dice`. Purpose: Damage Reduction Dice exists so damage subtraction is not flattened into utility prose or mis-modeled as resistance. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no rolled damage-reduction mechanic.

fields.label: Damage Reduction Applies To

log.why: Damage reduction needs to say whether it subtracts from incoming damage, outgoing damage, or another future damage total.

fields.comment: Names which damage total the reduction modifies. Current closed corpus usage is `damage_taken`, meaning the target subtracts the amount from incoming damage it takes. It maps to `effects[].damageReduction.appliesTo`. Purpose: Damage Reduction Applies To prevents reduction rules from being confused with attack damage bonuses or healing. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-reduction mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.damage_taken: Subtract from damage the protected target takes.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The spell has no damage-reduction mechanic.

fields.label: Damage Reduction Frequency

log.why: Resistance can benefit a creature only once per turn, so frequency must be typed instead of living only in prose.

fields.comment: Stores how often the damage reduction can be used while the effect is active. Use `once_per_turn` for prose like `only once per turn`; use `every_time` when every qualifying damage event is reduced. It maps to `effects[].damageReduction.frequency`. Purpose: Damage Reduction Frequency keeps consumption limits visible to runtime and validation. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-reduction mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.once_per_turn: Only one qualifying damage event per turn can be reduced.
acceptedValueNotes.every_time: Every qualifying damage event can be reduced.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The spell has no damage-reduction mechanic.

fields.label: Resistance Suppression Damage Types

log.why: Elemental Bane makes the target lose resistance to the caster's chosen damage type, which is neither normal damage reduction nor a defensive resistance granted by the spell.

fields.comment: Lists the damage types whose existing resistance is suppressed on the affected target. Use this when canonical prose says the target loses resistance to a damage type, such as Elemental Bane; do not use it for granting resistance, granting vulnerability, or subtracting a damage amount. It maps to `effects[].resistanceSuppression.damageType[]`. Purpose: Resistance Suppression Damage Types keeps resistance-removal mechanics from being hidden in description prose or inverted into defensive resistance data. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no resistance-suppression mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The spell does not suppress any existing damage resistance.

fields.label: Resistance Suppression Damage Type Source

log.why: Elemental Bane lists eligible damage types but suppresses resistance only to the one type chosen when the spell is cast.

fields.comment: Explains how the active suppressed damage type is selected from Resistance Suppression Damage Types. Use `listed` when every listed resistance is suppressed, `chosen_damage_type` when the caster or player chooses one eligible type, and `triggering_damage_type` when the active suppressed type is copied from a triggering damage event. It maps to `effects[].resistanceSuppression.damageTypeSource`. Purpose: Resistance Suppression Damage Type Source prevents eligible choices from being mistaken for simultaneous suppression of every listed resistance. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no resistance-suppression mechanic.

acceptedValueNotes.listed: Every listed resistance suppression type applies directly.
acceptedValueNotes.chosen_damage_type: The active resistance suppression type is selected from the listed options.
acceptedValueNotes.triggering_damage_type: The active resistance suppression type is copied from the triggering damage event.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The spell has no resistance-suppression mechanic.

fields.label: Damage Interaction Modes

log.why: Hallow's extra effect can grant either Resistance or Vulnerability in an area, so the template needs a neutral damage-interaction mode.

fields.comment: Lists area or mode-based changes to how affected creatures receive damage. Use `resistance` and `vulnerability` for Hallow-style extra effects; do not force vulnerability into Defense Type. It maps to `effects[].damageInteraction.modes[]`. Purpose: Damage Interaction Modes keeps beneficial and harmful damage interaction choices in one runtime shape. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-interaction mode.

fields.label: Damage Interaction Damage Types
fields.comment: Lists eligible damage types for a damage-interaction mode. For chosen-type modes, list the choice set and pair it with Damage Interaction Damage Type Source. It maps to `effects[].damageInteraction.damageType[]`. Purpose: Damage Interaction Damage Types prevents area-mode resistance or vulnerability from being hidden in prose. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-interaction mode.

fields.label: Damage Interaction Damage Type Source
fields.comment: Explains how the active damage type is selected for a damage-interaction mode. Use `chosen_damage_type` when the caster chooses one type from the eligible list. It maps to `effects[].damageInteraction.damageTypeSource`. Purpose: Damage Interaction Damage Type Source keeps eligible choices from being mistaken for simultaneous interaction with every listed type. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-interaction mode.

fields.label: Damage Interaction Subject Scope
fields.comment: Names which subjects the damage-interaction mode can affect. Use `chosen_creature_types` for Hallow wording like `creatures of any types you choose`. It maps to `effects[].damageInteraction.subjectScope`. Purpose: Damage Interaction Subject Scope keeps mode eligibility separate from the spell's point/area target. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-interaction mode.

fields.label: Damage Interaction Duration Scope
fields.comment: States when the damage-interaction mode applies. Use `while_in_area` for area-bound mechanics such as Hallow's Resistance and Vulnerability extra effects. It maps to `effects[].damageInteraction.durationScope`. Purpose: Damage Interaction Duration Scope prevents area-local interactions from being treated as permanent creature changes. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no damage-interaction mode.

fields.label: Defense Source Categories

log.why: Investiture of Stone limits its B/P/S resistance to damage from attacks, not every possible source of those damage types.

fields.comment: Lists the incoming source categories a defensive effect applies to. Use this when canonical prose limits a defense by source, such as `from attacks`; do not use target eligibility fields for this, because the spell target and the incoming damage source are different things. It maps to `effects[].defenseSourceFilter.sourceCategories[]`. Purpose: Defense Source Categories prevents source-limited resistance or immunity from becoming unconditional. Use `not_applicable` when the defense has no source-category restriction.

acceptedValueNotes.attack: The defense applies to incoming damage or effects from attacks.
acceptedValueNotes.spell: The defense applies to incoming damage or effects from spells.
acceptedValueNotes.effect: The defense applies to other named effects.
acceptedValueNotes.environment: The defense applies to environmental sources.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The defense has no source-category restriction.

fields.label: Defense Source Attack Magical Status

log.why: Investiture of Stone resists B/P/S damage only from nonmagical attacks, so magical-status filtering must be typed.

fields.comment: States whether an attack-source defense applies to magical attacks, nonmagical attacks, any attack, or no attack-source filter. Use this only for defenses whose canonical prose limits incoming attack sources by magical status. It maps to `effects[].defenseSourceFilter.attackMagicalStatus`. Purpose: Defense Source Attack Magical Status keeps nonmagical-attack resistance from being treated as blanket damage resistance. Use `not_applicable` when the defense has no attack magical-status restriction.

acceptedValueNotes.any: The defense applies to attacks regardless of magical status.
acceptedValueNotes.magical: The defense applies only to magical attacks.
acceptedValueNotes.nonmagical: The defense applies only to nonmagical attacks.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: The defense has no attack magical-status restriction.
